column5: rcalib is a composition of a linear non-decreasing function I and a piecewise-affine non-decreasing normalization kernel
column6: ALN invariant: If N_missing' >= N_missing then rcalib' >= rcalib; CI replay job: inject synthetic diagnostic counts and verify monotonicity

Statement: Averaging good and bad ingest windows does not produce an artificially low average rcalib
Mathematical Justification: On each linear segment of its piecewise definition rcalib is an affine function of the diagnostic counts; the overall piecewise-affine map is convex
Implementation: Used to justify window-averaging of rcalib in risk estimation and reporting

column6: Ensures compatibility of rsigma with other risk coordinates and with the R metric definition

Statement: Small changes in sensor uncertainty components do not cause arbitrarily large changes in rsigma
Mathematical Justification: The L2 norm is 1-Lipschitz with respect to its inputs; composing with min(1
column5: Essential for bounding the change in the Lyapunov residual ΔVt under perturbations of uncertainty

Statement: Vt_full is a valid discrete Lyapunov candidate function
Mathematical Justification: Vt_full is a sum of non-negative quadratic terms with non-negative weights and is zero only when all coordinates are zero
Implementation: Forms the basis for the safestep controller logic and for non-expansive risk trajectories

Statement: The safestep invariant prevents risk expansion outside the safe interior
Mathematical Justification: The controller enforces Vt_plus <= Vt when Vt > V_int
Implementation: ensuring the risk trajectory is non-increasing until it enters the safe region
column5: Encoded as an ALN invariant and exercised in controller test suites and CI simulations

Statement: High underlying risk (rcalib or rsigma) is never hidden by trust scalars
Mathematical Justification: R is defined as the maximum over all unscaled risk coordinates including rcalib and rsigma while trust scalars only multiply K and E
Implementation: Proven directly from the definitions of R and E = 1 - R; validated in CI by replay tests with degraded data quality

Statement: K_adj and E_adj are non-increasing functions of rcalib
Implementation: the partial derivatives dK_adj/dr_calib and dE_adj/dr_calib are non-positive whenever K_raw and E_raw are non-negative
column5: Provides the mathematical backbone for the “only down-credit” principle; enforced by CI checks that K_adj and E_adj never increase when rcalib is artificially raised
